title: bermudiana striata
side_effects: Bermudiana striata can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues. Ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure to its sap may lead to skin burns and blisters. In some cases, improper use of Bermudiana striata may also trigger respiratory problems, such as asthma attacks or bronchitis, particularly in individuals with pre-existing conditions. If not handled carefully, the plant's extracts may interact with certain medications, exacerbating health conditions or causing unforeseen complications.
